mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate over 3 months signals tightening labor market conditions and rising wage pressures, which compress corporate profit margins and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprice earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% as of 2016-10-01 indicates tight labor market
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative forward returns do not occur in > 55% of instances where UNRATE declines by > 0.2pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in retail sales signals accelerating consumer demand and economic momentum, which raises equity risk appetite and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 5-day forward returns occur in < 55% of instances where RSAFS increases by > 1.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 1.5%, further rate increases signal monetary tightening that raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors rotate away from equities.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.4% as of 2016-10-01, below trigger threshold
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in < 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses above 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in industrial production and a simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate signals demand-side weakness and recessionary pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors price in earnings contraction.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.226 as of 2016-09-01, UNRATE at 4.9% as of 2016-10-01
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in < 55% of instances where both INDPRO declines > 0.5% and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp simultaneously, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts over 2 months signals strengthening residential investment and forward consumer wealth effects, which produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors anticipate broader economic acceleration.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1047.0 thousand units as of 2016-09-01
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in < 55% of instances where HOUST increases by > 3% over 2 months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint acceleration in payroll employment growth and a decline in the unemployment rate signals robust labor market momentum and rising consumer confidence, which produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprice earnings growth upward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 200K month-over-month AND UNRATE declines > 0.1pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 144952.0 thousand as of 2016-10-01, UNRATE at 4.9%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in < 60% of instances where both PAYEMS increases > 200K and UNRATE declines > 0.1pp, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation measured by CPI rises above 2.5% year-over-year, further increases signal erosion of real returns and trigger expectations of future rate hikes, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors demand higher equity risk premiums.
regime_hint: inflation acceleration or monetary policy normalization
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in < 50% of instances where CPI year-over-year change exceeds 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A joint acceleration in real GDP growth and a rise in retail sales signals broad-based economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, which produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reprice earnings growth and multiple expansion upward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2.5% AND RSAFS increases > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16702.1B as of 2016-07-01, RSAFS at 465914M as of 2016-10-01
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in < 60% of instances where both GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized and RSAFS increases > 2% month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.
